name: Cabbage and kohlrabi salad
intro: looking for kohlrabi, and it isn’t very easy to find. Serve the salad alongside rich main courses, such as Very

ingredients.item: white cabbage (8 to 9 oz) large bunch of dill, roughly chopped (6 heaped tbsp)
dried whole sour cherries grated zest of 1 lemon
garlic clove, crushed salt and white pepper
wide and 2 inches long. Cut the cabbage into ¼-inch-thick strips.

method: Put all the ingredients, apart from the alfalfa sprouts, in a large mixing bowl. Use your hands to massage everything together for about a minute so the flavors mix and the lemon can soften the cabbage and cherries. Let the salad sit for about 10 minutes.
Add most of the alfalfa sprouts and mix well again with your hands. Taste and adjust the seasoning; you need a fair amount of salt to counteract the lemon.
Use your hands again to lift the salad out of the mixing bowl and into a serving bowl, leaving most of the juices behind. Garnish with the remaining sprouts and serve at once.

This salad will bring color to your winter dinner table and liven up any meal. You can leave out the caramelized macadamias, if you like, or use roasted peanuts instead. Consider serving the slaw with Chard
